description: Pain or discomfort in the head or face area including temples and forehead
advice: Rest in a quiet dark room, stay hydrated, take OTC pain relievers like acetaminophen or ibuprofen if needed, apply cold compress to forehead
red_flags: Sudden severe thunderclap headache;Vision changes or loss;Confusion or difficulty speaking;Fever with stiff neck;Weakness on one side
when_to_seek_help: Seek immediate help if headache is sudden and severe or accompanied by fever and stiff neck or neurological symptoms

description: Intense throbbing headache often on one side with nausea and light sensitivity
advice: Rest in dark quiet room, take prescribed migraine medication early, stay hydrated, avoid known triggers, consider caffeine in small amounts
red_flags: Worst headache of life;Vision loss;Weakness or numbness;Difficulty speaking;Fever
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if migraines become more frequent or severe or if you experience new neurological symptoms

description: Elevated body temperature above 38°C (100.4°F) indicating infection or inflammation
advice: Rest and stay hydrated with water and clear fluids, take fever-reducing medication like acetaminophen or ibuprofen, use light clothing and blankets
red_flags: Fever above 39.4°C (103°F);Difficulty breathing;Severe headache;Stiff neck;Rash;Confusion
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if fever persists more than 3 days or is very high or accompanied by severe symptoms

description: Reflex action to clear airways which can be dry or productive with mucus
advice: Stay hydrated, use honey for soothing (adults only), try cough drops or lozenges, use humidifier, rest voice
red_flags: Coughing blood;Difficulty breathing;Chest pain;High fever;Persistent cough over 3 weeks
when_to_seek_help: Seek medical attention if cough produces blood or is accompanied by difficulty breathing or persists beyond 2-3 weeks

description: Discomfort or pain in the chest area which can range from dull ache to sharp pain
advice: Stop all activity immediately, sit or lie down, call emergency services if severe, take aspirin if heart attack suspected and not allergic
red_flags: Pressure or squeezing sensation;Pain radiating to arm jaw or back;Shortness of breath;Sweating;Nausea
when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately if chest pain is severe or accompanied by shortness of breath or you suspect heart attack

description: Difficulty breathing or feeling like you cannot get enough air
advice: Sit upright and try to relax, use pursed lip breathing, remove tight clothing, use prescribed inhaler if available, call for help if severe
red_flags: Unable to speak in full sentences;Blue lips or fingertips;Severe wheezing;Chest pain;Confusion
when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services if you cannot catch your breath or have blue discoloration or chest pain

description: Persistent tiredness and lack of energy not relieved by rest
advice: Ensure adequate sleep of 7-9 hours, eat balanced meals, stay hydrated, exercise moderately, reduce stress, limit caffeine and alcohol
red_flags: Unexplained weight loss;Persistent fever;Severe weakness;Depression symptoms;Night sweats
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if fatigue persists more than 2 weeks or is accompanied by unexplained symptoms

description: Feeling of unease and discomfort in stomach with urge to vomit
advice: Eat small bland meals, stay hydrated with clear fluids, avoid strong odors, rest, try ginger or peppermint tea, avoid lying flat after eating
red_flags: Severe abdominal pain;Blood in vomit;Signs of dehydration;Persistent vomiting over 24 hours;Head injury
when_to_seek_help: Seek medical attention if vomiting persists over 24 hours or contains blood or you show signs of dehydration

description: Forceful expulsion of stomach contents through the mouth
advice: Stay hydrated with small sips of clear fluids, avoid solid foods initially, rest, try BRAT diet when able to eat (bananas rice applesauce toast)
red_flags: Blood in vomit;Severe abdominal pain;Signs of dehydration;Green or yellow vomit;Confusion
when_to_seek_help: Seek immediate care if vomiting blood or showing signs of severe dehydration or abdominal pain

description: Loose watery stools occurring more frequently than normal
advice: Stay hydrated with water and electrolyte solutions, eat bland foods, avoid dairy and fatty foods, rest, wash hands frequently
red_flags: Blood in stool;Severe abdominal pain;Signs of dehydration;Fever over 39°C;Symptoms lasting over 3 days
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if diarrhea contains blood or persists more than 3 days or you show dehydration signs

description: Discomfort or pain in the abdominal area ranging from mild to severe
advice: Rest, apply heat pad, eat bland foods, avoid spicy or fatty foods, stay hydrated, try antacids for mild symptoms
red_flags: Severe sudden pain;Pain with fever;Blood in stool or vomit;Unable to pass gas;Rigid abdomen
when_to_seek_help: Seek immediate care if pain is severe and sudden or accompanied by fever or blood

description: Pain in the lower middle or upper back often due to muscle strain or posture
advice: Rest but avoid prolonged bed rest, apply ice then heat, take OTC pain relievers, gentle stretching, maintain good posture
red_flags: Pain with numbness in legs;Loss of bladder or bowel control;Pain after injury;Severe pain at night;Unexplained weight loss
when_to_seek_help: Seek immediate care if you have numbness weakness or loss of bladder control with back pain

description: Pain stiffness or swelling in joints which may indicate arthritis or injury
advice: Rest the affected joint, apply ice for acute pain or heat for stiffness, take OTC anti-inflammatory medication, gentle movement, maintain healthy weight
red_flags: Severe swelling;Redness and warmth;Unable to move joint;Fever with joint pain;Joint deformity
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if joint pain is accompanied by fever severe swelling or inability to move the joint

description: Soreness or pain in muscles often from overuse strain or illness
advice: Rest affected muscles, apply ice for first 48 hours then heat, gentle stretching, take OTC pain relievers, stay hydrated, massage
red_flags: Severe pain after injury;Swelling with muscle pain;Dark urine;Weakness;High fever
when_to_seek_help: Seek medical attention if muscle pain is severe after injury or accompanied by dark urine or high fever

description: Feeling lightheaded faint or unsteady which can have many causes
advice: Sit or lie down immediately, stay hydrated, move slowly when changing positions, avoid sudden movements, avoid driving
red_flags: Severe headache;Vision changes;Chest pain;Difficulty speaking;Fainting
when_to_seek_help: Seek immediate care if dizziness is accompanied by chest pain difficulty speaking or severe headache

description: Pain scratchiness or irritation of the throat often worse when swallowing
advice: Gargle with warm salt water, drink warm fluids, use throat lozenges, stay hydrated, rest voice, use humidifier
red_flags: Difficulty breathing or swallowing;Drooling;Severe pain;High fever;Rash
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if you have difficulty breathing or swallowing or a fever over 38.3°C lasting more than 48 hours

description: Excess nasal discharge often due to cold allergies or sinus infection
advice: Stay hydrated, use saline nasal spray, use humidifier, rest, take antihistamines for allergies, decongestants for short term use
red_flags: Discharge lasting over 10 days;Green discharge with fever;Severe facial pain;Bloody discharge
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if symptoms persist over 10 days or you have severe facial pain or high fever

description: Change in skin color or texture including redness bumps or itching
advice: Keep area clean and dry, avoid scratching, apply calamine lotion or hydrocortisone cream, take antihistamines for itching, identify and avoid triggers
red_flags: Rapid spreading;Fever with rash;Blisters;Difficulty breathing;Swelling of face or throat
when_to_seek_help: Seek immediate care if rash spreads rapidly is accompanied by fever or you have difficulty breathing

description: Excessive worry fear or nervousness affecting daily life
advice: Practice deep breathing exercises, try progressive muscle relaxation, limit caffeine and alcohol, exercise regularly, maintain sleep schedule, consider therapy
red_flags: Panic attacks;Thoughts of self-harm;Unable to function daily;Chest pain;Severe symptoms
when_to_seek_help: Seek help immediately if you have thoughts of self-harm or panic attacks that feel like heart attacks

description: Difficulty falling asleep staying asleep or waking too early
advice: Maintain consistent sleep schedule, avoid screens before bed, limit caffeine especially after noon, create dark quiet sleep environment, exercise during day not evening
red_flags: Severe daytime impairment;Symptoms over 3 weeks;Sleep apnea signs;Depression symptoms
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if insomnia persists more than 3 weeks or significantly affects daily functioning

description: Red or bloodshot appearance of the white of the eye
advice: Apply cool compress, use artificial tears, rest eyes from screens, avoid rubbing eyes, remove contact lenses if wearing
red_flags: Severe eye pain;Vision changes;Sensitivity to light;Discharge;Eye injury
when_to_seek_help: Seek immediate care if you have severe eye pain vision changes or injury to the eye

description: Pain or discomfort in the ear which may indicate infection
advice: Apply warm compress to ear, take OTC pain relievers, keep ear dry, try OTC ear drops if no perforation suspected
red_flags: Severe pain;Hearing loss;Discharge from ear;High fever;Dizziness
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if ear pain is severe accompanied by hearing loss discharge or high fever

description: Pain in or around a tooth often indicating decay or infection
advice: Rinse with warm salt water, take OTC pain relievers, apply cold compress to cheek, avoid very hot or cold foods, see dentist promptly
red_flags: Severe pain;Fever;Swelling in face or jaw;Difficulty swallowing or breathing;Pus
when_to_seek_help: Seek immediate dental or medical care if you have facial swelling difficulty breathing or high fever with tooth pain

description: Immune response to allergen causing various symptoms
advice: Remove allergen exposure, take antihistamines, apply cold compress for skin reactions, use prescribed epinephrine if severe and available
red_flags: Difficulty breathing;Swelling of face throat or tongue;Rapid pulse;Dizziness or fainting;Widespread hives
when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately if you have difficulty breathing facial swelling or signs of anaphylaxis

description: Infrequent bowel movements or difficulty passing stool
advice: Increase fiber intake with fruits vegetables and whole grains, drink plenty of water, exercise regularly, try OTC fiber supplements or stool softeners
red_flags: Severe abdominal pain;Blood in stool;Unexplained weight loss;Alternating with diarrhea;No bowel movement over 7 days
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if constipation is accompanied by severe pain blood in stool or persists despite home treatment

description: Feeling of fullness or swelling in the abdomen
advice: Eat slowly and chew thoroughly, avoid carbonated drinks, limit salt intake, try peppermint tea, walk after meals, avoid foods that cause gas
red_flags: Severe pain;Vomiting;Blood in stool;Unexplained weight loss;Persistent bloating over 2 weeks
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if bloating is severe persistent or accompanied by other concerning symptoms

description: Burning sensation in chest behind breastbone often after eating
advice: Avoid trigger foods like spicy fatty or acidic foods, eat smaller meals, don't lie down after eating, elevate head when sleeping, try OTC antacids
red_flags: Difficulty swallowing;Persistent vomiting;Unexplained weight loss;Chest pain with exertion;Black stools
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if heartburn is frequent or accompanied by difficulty swallowing weight loss or severe symptoms

description: Elevated blood pressure readings often without symptoms
advice: Reduce salt intake, exercise regularly, maintain healthy weight, limit alcohol, manage stress, take prescribed medications, monitor blood pressure regularly
red_flags: Severe headache;Vision problems;Chest pain;Difficulty breathing;Blood in urine
when_to_seek_help: Seek immediate care if blood pressure is severely elevated or accompanied by chest pain vision problems or severe headache

description: Blood glucose below normal levels causing various symptoms
advice: Eat or drink fast-acting sugar like glucose tablets juice or regular soda, recheck blood sugar after 15 minutes, eat a meal or snack after recovery
red_flags: Confusion;Seizures;Loss of consciousness;Unable to swallow;Severe symptoms
when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services if person is unconscious having seizures or unable to swallow

description: Infection in urinary system causing burning urination and frequency
advice: Drink plenty of water, urinate frequently, take prescribed antibiotics if given, use OTC pain relief for symptoms, apply heating pad to abdomen
red_flags: Fever;Back or side pain;Blood in urine;Nausea or vomiting;Symptoms spreading
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor promptly for antibiotics and seek immediate care if you have fever back pain or blood in urine

description: Pain in lower abdomen during menstruation
advice: Apply heat to lower abdomen, take OTC pain relievers like ibuprofen, exercise lightly, try relaxation techniques, rest
red_flags: Severe pain not relieved by medication;Heavy bleeding soaking pad hourly;Fever;Pain between periods
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if cramps are severe interfere with daily activities or are accompanied by heavy bleeding

description: Viral respiratory infection with congestion cough and body aches
advice: Rest and stay home, stay hydrated, take OTC cold medications for symptom relief, use humidifier, gargle salt water for sore throat
red_flags: High fever over 39.4°C;Difficulty breathing;Chest pain;Confusion;Severe symptoms
when_to_seek_help: Seek medical attention if symptoms are severe you have difficulty breathing or symptoms worsen after initial improvement

description: Respiratory illness caused by coronavirus with varied symptoms
advice: Isolate from others, rest, stay hydrated, monitor symptoms and oxygen levels if available, take OTC medications for fever and pain
red_flags: Difficulty breathing;Persistent chest pain;Confusion;Inability to stay awake;Bluish lips or face
when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services if you have trouble breathing persistent chest pain confusion or bluish discoloration

description: Insufficient fluid in body from illness heat or inadequate intake
advice: Drink water and electrolyte solutions slowly, rest in cool environment, eat water-rich foods, avoid caffeine and alcohol
red_flags: Extreme thirst;Very dark urine;Dizziness or confusion;Rapid heartbeat;No urination for 8 hours
when_to_seek_help: Seek medical care if you have signs of severe dehydration like confusion rapid heartbeat or inability to keep fluids down

description: Body overheating from hot conditions and dehydration
advice: Move to cool shaded area, remove excess clothing, apply cool water to skin, drink cool water slowly, rest
red_flags: Temperature over 40°C;Confusion;Seizures;Not sweating despite heat;Loss of consciousness
when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately if symptoms progress to heat stroke with high temperature or confusion

description: Skin damage from UV radiation causing redness and pain
advice: Stay out of sun, apply cool compresses, use aloe vera gel, take OTC pain relievers, stay hydrated, wear loose clothing
red_flags: Severe blistering;Fever;Chills;Nausea;Confusion
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if sunburn is severe with blistering covers large area or is accompanied by fever

description: Stretching or tearing of ligaments around a joint
advice: Rest injured area, apply ice for 20 minutes several times daily, compress with bandage, elevate above heart, take OTC pain relievers
red_flags: Severe pain;Unable to bear weight;Significant swelling;Numbness;Visible deformity
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if you cannot bear weight have severe swelling or suspect a fracture

description: Stretching or tearing of muscle or tendon
advice: Rest injured muscle, apply ice initially then heat after 48 hours, gentle stretching after acute phase, take OTC pain relievers
red_flags: Severe pain;Significant bruising;Unable to move affected area;Pop felt during injury
when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if strain is severe you cannot move the affected area or suspect a tear
